items.title: NT diphtheria cases declining as 10,000 immunised, one death confirmed

children.content: The Northern Territory government says the rate of new diphtheria cases in the territory has fallen from more than 20 per week to less than 10 after 10,000 Territorians stepped up to be immunised against the ongoing outbreak.

articles.title: How to check if your vaccines are up to date as diphtheria outbreak continues

children.content: Do you know your vaccination history? Some vaccines need to be updated regularly, especially if you are travelling. Here's what you need to know to ensure you stay protected.

image.altText: A health worker puts a bandaid over a spot where someone was vaccinated.

articles.title: Aboriginal community workers say diphtheria response 'heartbreaking'

children.content: Residents and service providers in the remote community of Yuendumu, where there are reports of dozens of diphtheria cases, say the NT government has not done enough to keep people safe.

image.altText: a cow standing near a fence with a hill in the background

articles.title: NT Health confirms only one possible diphtheria-related death amid outbreak

children.content: The Northern Territory health department is refuting an earlier report that a second person has died with diphtheria in the NT, amid Australia's largest outbreak of the disease since records began.

items.title: Man found not guilty of murdering Darwin mother a 'serious risk' to community

children.content: Sheena Fairfield was brutally stabbed to death in the yard of her suburban home in 2022. The man charged with her murder never went to trial due to mental impairment but he could be detained for 20 years.
